source.description: Six-panel generated raster process sheet showing one beach umbrella badge from construction to finished marker drawing.
source.notes: Subject, pose, and final elements were locked before image generation for the June 10, 2026 honest backfill lesson.

process_strategy: The umbrella canopy, scallops, pole, panel seams, marker colors, and sticker shadow are all introduced before the final cleanup pass.

final_elements.name: rounded umbrella canopy
final_elements.description: A broad half-circle canopy tilted slightly like a sticker badge.

final_elements.name: scalloped lower edge
final_elements.description: Soft scallops along the bottom of the canopy.

final_elements.name: center pole and knob
final_elements.description: A simple pole below the canopy and a small top knob.

final_elements.name: radiating panel seams
final_elements.description: Curved panel seam lines spreading from the top knob to the scallops.

final_elements.name: bright marker panel colors
final_elements.description: Alternating turquoise, coral, and yellow marker fills on existing canopy panels.

final_elements.name: gray sticker shadow
final_elements.description: A small offset shadow behind the already drawn umbrella.

frames.visible_job: Draws the rounded canopy guide as one broad half-circle.
Adds the scalloped lower canopy edge and starts the thick outer line.
Adds the center pole and top knob to the existing canopy.
Adds curved panel seams from the knob down to the scalloped edge.
Adds alternating bright marker fills, white shine gaps, marker streaks, and a gray sticker shadow to existing shapes.

final_step.step_name: Snap the umbrella badge into place
final_step.visible_job: Thickens the existing outlines and sharpens the already present panels, fills, shine gaps, pole, and shadow.

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, ink, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
